lesson: Every run: resurface one past lesson and re-check it before the gate.
Body text contrast must be >=4.5:1 (WCAG AA). Light-gray-on-white is the most common failure.
Icon-only buttons need an aria-label, or axe flags button-name (critical).

Emoji inside a button SATISFIES axe's button-name rule. The gate goes green while a screen reader announces 'key'. Use an aria-hidden inline SVG for icons so a missing aria-label is actually catchable.

change: run axe at 375px and 1280px, not just default 1280x720
lesson: axe analyzes whatever viewport is current. Running it once before any setViewportSize only ever tested desktop. Mobile-only contrast/naming failures passed silently.

change: lift muted contrast to #5b6470; add aria-label to provider button
lesson: #b8b8b8 on white is 1.9:1. Nowhere near AA. Muted greys need ~#5b6470 minimum on a white card to clear 4.5:1.

change: capture viewport screenshots for the visual record
lesson: A design loop that logs only text cannot show you the design. Capture the viewport at each tested width; fullPage crops badly on centred layouts.

change: pixel-first focus check with disclosed computed-style fallback
lesson: Playwright waits for element STABILITY; a page with continuous motion (WebGL canvas) costs ~12s per element and animations:'disabled' only freezes CSS. Cap the timeout and fall back, but SAY which method ran.

change: split 'hover uses more than colour' into affordance + colour-only
lesson: A check phrased as 'is X done badly?' passes vacuously when X is absent entirely. Ask 'does X exist?' first, then 'is it done well?'

change: measurements briefed to the auditor but never marked settled
lesson: Telling a judge a measurement is settled removes the only independent check on the measurer. Brief it, invite challenge, and run blind periodically as the control.

change: extracted invariants from a published design spec instead of porting it
lesson: A spec's specifics are brand (#0066ff, Messina, J/K); only the reason behind them transfers. Port the invariant, discard the instance.

change: measure hierarchy on all four levers instead of judging it whole
lesson: Hierarchy = size + weight + contrast + spacing, and it is a RELATIONSHIP. Measure differences, not values. A closed set of sizes proves nothing: 12/13/14px is three values and one rank because the steps are under 1.1x.

change: workbench shell made durable: assert nav + panel exist AND the toggles change state; panel drag-to-resize
lesson: A control that renders but does nothing passes a PRESENCE check vacuously. A durable UI guard must exercise BEHAVIOUR (click it, assert state changed), not just query that the element exists. Same shape as CF-065: affordance present is not affordance working.

change: built the design doctrine store (contrarian+evidence gate, review-date anti-stale) under a full phased Trident run
lesson: A gate that PRINTS "FAIL" but EXITS 0 is not wired. The trigger must propagate the exit code (--strict), or CI stays green on input the gate already rejected. The cold audit caught this; presence of a check is not enforcement of it.
